page: Microsoft Azure D16ds_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Ddsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It includes 945 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-threaded performance, with gzip compression reaching 35.66 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.92 GB/sec, both placing the server in the top tier. Multi-threaded decompression and general CPU benchmarks, such as stress-ng and PassMark, fall within the average range. The server demonstrates solid memory bandwidth, recording 1217.74 GB/sec for small block cached reads and 129.38 GB/sec for large block uncached reads. For language model tasks, text generation using a small 135M model achieves a top-tier speed of 354.35 tokens/sec, while larger models perform at average speeds.

Given its balanced vCPU-to-memory ratio and the inclusion of a 945 GB local disk, this instance offers a cost-effective profile for workloads requiring a mix of compute, memory, and local storage. The hardware is well-suited for general-purpose applications, relational databases, web serving, and moderate data processing tasks. The presence of advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX also supports specialized vector and matrix math operations, making it suitable for lightweight machine learning inference and cryptographic workloads.
description: Microsoft Azure D16ds_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Ddsv7 family, powered by a 16-vCPU Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 64.0 GB of RAM, 945 GB of local storage, and supports Premium Storage. The server lacks dedicated GPUs. In benchmark testing, the D16ds_v7 displays top-tier single-threaded performance, with gzip compression at 35.66 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.92 GB/sec. It also achieves top-tier text generation speeds of 354.35 tokens/sec for small language models, while maintaining average scores in multi-core processing, database operations, and memory bandwidth. This balanced resource profile makes the instance highly suitable for general-purpose workloads, web serving, database management, and lightweight AI inference tasks that benefit from its local storage and advanced Intel instruction sets.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16ds_v7 is a general-purpose VM featuring 16 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 945 GB local storage. It delivers top-tier single-threaded compression and small-model LLM text generation performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16ds_v7 is a 16-vCPU general-purpose VM with 64 GB RAM, 945 GB local storage, and top-tier single-threaded compression performance.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring high-speed Intel Xeon processing, substantial local storage, and exceptional single-threaded compression capabilities.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs and 64 GB RAM on an Intel Xeon 4.2 GHz processor
945 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression speeds
Excellent text generation performance for small-scale language models
Supports advanced vector and matrix extensions including AVX-512 and AMX
Optimized for general-purpose applications, databases, and web serving
